name: Bronzed Chicken Wings with Young Ginger
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the wing tips off the chicken wings.
2. Place the chicken wings in a shallow baking dish.
3. In a small bowl, stir together corn syrup and soy sauce. Pour over the chicken wings; toss to coat well. Marinate for 30 minutes. Drain; reserve the marinade.
4. Heat corn oil in a large heavy skillet over medium heat. Add the chicken wings and ginger; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
5. Stir in the reserved marinade, sherry, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and green onions. Stir frequently and cook for 2 minutes.
6. Add chicken broth. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes or until tender. Remove the chicken wings from the skillet and keep warm.
7. In a small bowl, stir together cornstarch and water until smooth. Stir into the skillet. Stirring constantly, bring to a boil over medium heat and boil for 1 minute.
8. Spoon the sauce over the chicken wings.
9. Makes 4 servings.
